reference_answer: The pilot's failure to set collective friction which resulted in collective bounce and subsequent loss of control.
supporting_source_spans: Probable Cause and Findings: Probable Cause

reference_answer: Collective bounce is a pilot-induced vertical oscillation of the collective control system when absolute friction is less than seven pounds, characterized by rapid buildup at approximately three cycles per second.
supporting_source_spans: Aircraft Systems Information: Operators manual quotation about collective bounce

reference_answer: Seven pounds of absolute friction is the threshold; below this level, collective bounce may be encountered.
supporting_source_spans: Aircraft Systems Information: 'absolute friction (either pilot applied, or control rigged) is less than seven pounds'

reference_answer: Personnel issues: Use of equipment/system - Pilot; Personnel issues: Aircraft control - Pilot
supporting_source_spans: Probable Cause and Findings: Findings section

reference_answer: Operator claimed 'there were no preaccident mechanical malfunctions or failures that would have precluded normal operations.' NTSB probable cause identified 'the pilot's failure to set collective friction' - not a mechanical malfunction but a pilot error.
supporting_source_spans: Narrative: 'The operator reported that there were no preaccident mechanical malfunctions...'
Probable Cause: 'The pilot's failure to set collective friction...'

reference_answer: The note states 'The NTSB did not travel to the scene of this accident.' Investigation Class: Class 4.
supporting_source_spans: Administrative Information: Note at bottom, Investigation Class: Class 4

reference_answer: Clear skies, 10 miles visibility, 3 knots wind from 130 degrees, temperature 21°C / dew point 12°C, no precipitation or turbulence.
supporting_source_spans: Meteorological Information and Flight Plan table: all weather fields

reference_answer: The pilot should apply and maintain adequate collective friction in all flight conditions to prevent collective bounce.
supporting_source_spans: Aircraft Systems Information: Operators manual quotation: 'The pilot should apply and maintain adequate collective friction in all flight conditions.'
